steps.title: Solve the system by elimination
steps.content: Add the two equations to eliminate $y$: $$0=13x-6,$$ so $13x=6$.

steps.title: Find $39x$
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $3$: $$39x=3\cdot 13x=18.$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. Adding the second equation to the first equation in the given system of equations yields $3y-3y=4x+9x+17-23$, or $0=13x-6$. Adding $6$ to each side of this equation yields $6=13x$. Multiplying each side of this equation by $3$ yields $18=39x$. Therefore, the value of $39x$ is $18$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $-39x$, not $39x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $-13x$, not $39x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $13x$, not $39x$.
